questions.q: How many akshauhinis in total assembled for the war at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Eighteen akshauhinis gathered on the field, mirroring the war's eighteen days and the epic's eighteen books.

questions.q: How many akshauhinis fought on the Pandava side?
questions.explanation: The Pandavas fielded seven akshauhinis against the Kauravas' larger host.

questions.q: How many akshauhinis did Duryodhana's Kaurava alliance field?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana assembled eleven akshauhinis, outnumbering the Pandava force of seven.

questions.q: How many foot soldiers marched in a single akshauhini?
questions.explanation: Each akshauhini contained exactly 109,350 infantry, the largest of its four arms.

questions.q: How many chariots rolled in one akshauhini?
questions.explanation: An akshauhini held 21,870 chariots, each typically carrying a warrior and his driver.

questions.q: In an akshauhini, the count of elephants exactly equals the count of what?
questions.explanation: Elephants and chariots match precisely at 21,870 each in the akshauhini's fixed arithmetic.

questions.q: How many horsemen rode in one akshauhini?
questions.explanation: Each akshauhini included 65,610 cavalry, three times its number of chariots.

questions.q: What is an akshauhini, as the epic uses the term?
options: A royal bodyguard of picked champions
A fortress garrison of a great city
A fleet of war chariots fighting alone
A full army division of chariots, elephants, horse, and foot
questions.explanation: An akshauhini is a complete battle formation combining all four classical arms in fixed proportions.

questions.q: Which side enjoyed clear numerical superiority at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The Kauravas' eleven akshauhinis outnumbered the Pandavas' seven, yet the smaller army prevailed.

questions.q: What became of the eighteen akshauhinis after the eighteen days of battle?
options: They defected to the victorious side
questions.explanation: Of the millions who assembled, only a tiny handful of warriors on both sides outlived the war.

questions.q: What fixed ratio binds chariots, elephants, horses, and foot soldiers in an akshauhini?
questions.explanation: For every chariot there is one elephant, three horses, and five foot soldiers, scaled up through every unit.

questions.q: What is the smallest unit of the army, holding one chariot, one elephant, three horses, and five foot soldiers?
questions.explanation: The patti is the basic building block from which all larger formations up to the akshauhini are multiplied.

questions.q: When Krishna offered one side his Narayani Sena and the other only his unarmed self, who took the army?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana gleefully claimed the akshauhini of Narayana warriors, thinking he had the better bargain.

questions.q: Who chose the unarmed Krishna instead of his entire fighting force?
questions.explanation: Arjuna asked only for Krishna himself, even weaponless, and gained the war's true deciding force.

questions.q: Each Kaurava akshauhini had its own general, but who was named first supreme commander over all eleven?
questions.explanation: Bhishma led the eleven akshauhinis for the first ten days before Drona, Karna, and Shalya succeeded him.

questions.q: Who commanded the Pandavas' seven akshauhinis as supreme general for all eighteen days?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna, the flame-born prince of Panchala, held supreme command of the Pandava host throughout the war.

questions.q: The digits of 21,870, of 65,610, and of 109,350 each add up to which number beloved of the epic?
questions.explanation: Every figure in the akshauhini sums to eighteen, echoing the eighteen days, armies, and books of the Mahabharata.

questions.q: Where in the Mahabharata is the akshauhini's exact composition spelled out?
options: The Bhishma Parva, before the first day's fighting
The Udyoga Parva's councils of war
The Adi Parva, at the epic's very opening
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva lays out the precise arithmetic of the akshauhini before the story of the war is told.

questions.q: Which Yadava hero brought an akshauhini of troops to the Pandava cause?
questions.explanation: Satyaki marched to Yudhishthira with one akshauhini, while Kritavarma led Yadava forces to Duryodhana.

questions.q: Adding chariots, elephants, horsemen, and foot soldiers, how many fighting units does one akshauhini contain?
questions.explanation: The four arms total 218,700 units, before even counting drivers, attendants, and camp followers.

questions.q: The akshauhini multiplies which classical fourfold model of the Indian army?
questions.explanation: The chaturanga, the four-limbed army of chariots, elephants, cavalry, and infantry, later gave its name to chess.

questions.q: Which king led an akshauhini from distant Kamboja to fight for Duryodhana?
questions.explanation: Sudakshina of Kamboja brought an akshauhini from the far northwest to swell the Kaurava ranks.

questions.q: In the army arithmetic of the Adi Parva, three pattis together form which unit?
questions.explanation: Three pattis make a senamukha, the second rung on the ladder that climbs to the full akshauhini.

questions.q: How many anikinis combine to make one full akshauhini?
questions.explanation: Unlike the threefold steps below it, the final step multiplies by ten: ten anikinis equal one akshauhini.

questions.q: What death toll did Yudhishthira report to Dhritarashtra when asked how many had fallen in the war?
questions.explanation: In the Stree Parva Yudhishthira counts one billion, 660 million and 20,000 men slain in the eighteen days.

questions.q: Of all the warriors in the eleven Kaurava akshauhinis, how many lived to see the war's end?
questions.explanation: Only Ashwatthama, Kripacharya, and Kritavarma survived on the Kaurava side, the trio of the night raid.

questions.q: Who commanded Krishna's Narayani Sena on the Kaurava side at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The Yadava chief Kritavarma led the Narayani Sena for Duryodhana and survived to join the night raid.

questions.q: Bhagadatta of Pragjyotisha brought his forces to the Kauravas riding which famous war elephant?
questions.explanation: Bhagadatta fought from the mighty Supratika until Arjuna brought down both king and elephant on the twelfth day.

questions.q: Besides the dead, how many warriors did Yudhishthira say were missing and unaccounted for?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira's reckoning in the Stree Parva adds 24,165 warriors of whom nothing more was known.

questions.q: Who recites the akshauhini's precise arithmetic in the epic's opening frame at the Naimisha forest?
questions.explanation: The bard Ugrashrava Sauti details the numbers of the akshauhini to Shaunaka's sages in the Adi Parva.
